title: quercus × dysophylla
intro: Quercus × dysophylla, also known as the hybrid oak, is a rare deciduous tree that results from the cross between Quercus robur and Quercus petraea. The bark and leaves of the Quercus × dysophylla have been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly for their astringent and antiseptic properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus × dysophylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight infections, and support the immune system, making it a potential remedy for various health issues such as wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of Quercus × dysophylla may include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to take advantage of its health benefits.
